title: NOC 14201 – Banking, Insurance, and Other Financial Clerks | GTR Immigration
description: Banking, Insurance, and Other Financial Clerks play a vital role in supporting daily operations within financial institutions, insurance providers, and investment firms across Canada. Classified under NOC 14201, this occupation has experienced strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—offering realistic immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Growth in consumer lending and insurance servicesExpansion of financial compliance operationsEntry-level TEER occupation with advancement potentialEmployer-supported recruitmentIncreased demand for customer-facing financial roles
classification.major_group: 142 – Financial, insurance, and related administrative support workers
classification.minor_group: 1420 – Financial, insurance, and related administrative support workers

duties: Bank Clerks
Bank Clerks may:
Compile records of deposits, withdrawals, loan payments, securities transactions, and cheques
Process loan and mortgage applications, drafts, money orders, and term deposits
Balance ATM transactions and ledger entries
Calculate service charges and interest payments
Answer enquiries about banking products and services
Sell foreign currency, travellers’ cheques, and rent safety-deposit boxes
Open and close accounts
Insurance Clerks
Insurance Clerks may:
Process enrolments, cancellations, claims, and policy changes
Verify coverage, premiums, and client information
Calculate premiums, pension benefits, and annuity payments
Maintain insurance records and claims data
Respond to customer enquiries
Other Financial Clerks
Other Financial Clerks may:
Maintain real estate and securities listings
Process financial transactions
Sort and verify documentation
Reply to correspondence and customer enquiries
employment_requirements: Short-term training programs related to financial institutions
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate approximately 12,900 new positions for Banking, Insurance, and Other Financial Clerks, while around 12,700 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Banking, Insurance, and Other Financial Clerks are in demand across both public and private financial sectors nationwide. Hiring has expanded steadily since the pandemic and is projected to continue over the coming years.
Banks and credit unionsInsurance companies (public and private)Investment and brokerage firmsMortgage lendersReal estate officesFinancial service providers
Annual salaries typically range between $32,600 and $67,100 CAD, depending on province, experience, and specialization.
